terms.one_liner: A lightweight way to fine tune a large AI model.
terms.deeper: LoRA trains small adapter matrices instead of changing every model weight. In this transcript, it matters because it lowers the cost and memory needed for fine tuning.
terms.quote: LoRA, which stands for Low-Rank Adaptation

terms.one_liner: A method that gives a model retrieved context before it answers.
terms.deeper: The system looks up relevant source material and includes it in the prompt. In this transcript, it is compared with supervised fine tuning as a different way to improve model behavior.

terms.one_liner: A training technique that randomly disables parts of a neural network.
terms.deeper: Dropout can reduce overfitting during training, but it is not present in the sample transcript. This fixture keeps one false positive visible for evaluation tests.
